description: Fix documentation discrepancies found during audit: add 2 missing browser tools, update stale tool count from 37 to 39, and fix incorrect concurrency behavior description in MCP server docs.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add missing browser tools and update Network Capture section
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the browser tools reference to document all available network capture tools so that I can discover and use WebSocket frame capture and HAR export features.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_websocket_frames appears in the Network Capture table in browser-tools.mdx with description: 'Get WebSocket frames captured since browser_enable_network_capture was called on this tab. Each frame includes URL, direction (sent/received), data payload, opcode, and timestamp.'

browser_export_har appears in the Network Capture table in browser-tools.mdx with description: 'Export captured network traffic as a HAR 1.2 JSON file. Requires an active or completed capture session from browser_enable_network_capture.'
browser_get_websocket_frames row is placed after browser_get_network_requests and before browser_disable_network_capture
browser_export_har row is placed after browser_get_websocket_frames and before browser_disable_network_capture
browser_enable_network_capture description updated to mention WebSocket frame capture (e.g., 'Also captures WebSocket frame payloads — retrieve them with browser_get_websocket_frames.')
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx.

The Network Capture section starts at line 164. The table body has 3 rows (lines 176-188): browser_enable_network_capture, browser_get_network_requests, browser_disable_network_capture.

Add 2 new Table.Row entries after browser_get_network_requests (line 183) and before browser_disable_network_capture (line 184):

1. browser_get_websocket_frames — Source description from platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-websocket-frames.ts
2. browser_export_har — Source description from platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/export-har.ts

Also update the browser_enable_network_capture description (line 178) to add mention of WebSocket frames. The source description says: 'Also captures WebSocket frame payloads (sent and received) — retrieve them with browser_get_websocket_frames.'

Follow the existing table row pattern:
```
<Table.Row>
  <Table.Cell className="font-medium">`browser_get_websocket_frames`</Table.Cell>
  <Table.Cell>Description here.</Table.Cell>
</Table.Row>
```

userStories.title: Update browser tool count from 37 to 39 across all docs
userStories.description: As a user reading the documentation, I want the browser tool count to be accurate so that I know how many tools are available.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx frontmatter description says '39' instead of '37'
docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx summary table Total row says 39 instead of 37
docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx summary table Network Capture row says 5 instead of 3
docs/content/docs/index.mdx says '39 built-in browser tools' instead of '37'
docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx says '39 built-in browser tools' instead of '37'
docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx says '39 built-in browser tools' instead of '37'
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Simple find-and-replace across 4 files. All paths are repo-root-relative.

1. docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx:
   - Line 3: change '37 built-in browser tools' to '39 built-in browser tools' in frontmatter description
   - Line 329: Network Capture count cell — change 3 to 5
   - Line 354: Total count cell — change 37 to 39

2. docs/content/docs/index.mdx:
   - Line 33: change '37 built-in browser tools' to '39 built-in browser tools'

3. docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx:
   - Line 85: change '37 built-in browser tools' to '39 built-in browser tools'

4. docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx:
   - Line 75: change '37 built-in browser tools' to '39 built-in browser tools'

userStories.title: Fix concurrent dispatch description in MCP server docs
userStories.description: As a developer integrating with the MCP server, I want the dispatch pipeline documentation to accurately describe what happens when the concurrency limit is exceeded so that I can handle errors correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx no longer says 'additional calls are queued'
docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx says that additional calls beyond 5 concurrent per plugin return an error (not queued)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx.

Line 525 currently reads:
'Timeouts: 30 seconds by default, extended by progress reports (up to 5 minutes maximum). Up to 5 concurrent tool calls per plugin; additional calls are queued.'

Change the last clause to reflect the actual source behavior. The source code in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts lines 365-374 returns an error response with isError: true and message 'Too many concurrent dispatches for plugin "<name>" (limit: 5). Wait for in-flight requests to complete.'

Suggested replacement:
'Timeouts: 30 seconds by default, extended by progress reports (up to 5 minutes maximum). Up to 5 concurrent tool calls per plugin; additional calls return an error asking the client to wait for in-flight requests to complete.'
